title: Accessible Names, Images, Language, Headings, Landmarks, and Human Review · Stakeholder Software Mission
summary: A public-service content, search, localization, and accessibility team. They need repeatable canonical, indexing, hreflang, structured-data, image, heading, language, accessible-name, and changed-content evidence with clear automated limits and human-review handoff. Planned artifact: Deliver Incremental Accessible Search and Site-Quality Auditor with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, change…
objectives: Classify transport, metadata, template, content, accessibility, structured-data, and extraction-rule changes with prior and current evidence.
Reconcile redirects, HTTP canonical links, HTML canonical links, sitemap entries, internal links, and content identity without forcing agreement.
Parse page-level robots meta and X-Robots-Tag directives by applicable user agent and separate crawling from indexing and serving.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Automated Accessibility Lim…
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: scraper-automated-accessibility-limit-17yiav" that uses case 7648 to audit language, title, structure, images, names, and relationships with evidence and mandatory human review. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Automated Accessibility Lim….
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Automated Accessibility Limit into observable performance evidence. For Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Automated Accessibility Lim…, keep Automated Accessibility Limit connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-automated-accessibility-limit-17yiav
# Competency: scraper-automated-accessibility-limit.
# Case 7648: an authorized nonprofit content audit.
# Operating constraint: the target owner must be able to pause the crawl.
# Changed case: replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget.
def evidence_scraper_automated_accessibility_limit_17yiav(pages: int = 24, automated_findings: int = 38, manual_checks: int = 42, conformance_claims: int = 0, findings_with_evidence: int = 38) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_17yiav = "17yiav-vaiy71-7648"
    assert evidence_variant_17yiav
    evidence_axes_17yiav = (
        "authorization17yiav",
        "url7648",
        "responsevaiy71",
        "tree17yiav7648",
        "frontier764817yiav",
        "reportvaiy717648",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_17yiav)) == 6
    if pages < 1 or automated_findings < 0:
        return False, pages, "accessibility-count"
    if findings_with_evidence != automated_findings:
        return False, findings_with_evidence, "finding-evidence"
    if manual_checks < pages:
        return False, manual_checks, "manual-review-gap"
    if conformance_claims > 0:
        return False, conformance_claims, "conformance-overclaim"
    assert findings_with_evidence == automated_findings and conformance_claims == 0
    return True, automated_findings, "bounded-accessibility-audit"
